reasoning: The Lean statement faithfully encodes all components of the informal claim. The metric space `X`, the sets `K, F : Set X`, and the three hypotheses `IsCompact K`, `IsClosed F`, `Disjoint K F` directly correspond to "K and F are disjoint sets in a metric space X, K compact, F closed". The conclusion `∃ δ : ℝ, δ > 0 ∧ ∀ p q : X, p ∈ K → q ∈ F → dist p q > δ` matches exactly "there exists δ > 0 such that d(p,q) > δ if p ∈ K, q ∈ F", preserving the strict inequality and the universal quantification over the two sets. No hypotheses are added, dropped, or altered, and no types are changed.
